How's it going everyone? In this video I'm going to show you how to import the ad funnels that we give you as an example for cold traffic. Cold traffic is just ads that go directly to this funnel. It's also called external funnels. Now we're going to show you how to set up the domain and the path to where the link for it is going to look great and this is what you're actually going to use in your Facebook ads to point it to it. Now in this one we do have two different cold traffic funnels you can use as an example. We have the six-week 42-day challenge traffic funnel right there. We also have the 42-day detox cold traffic funnel. To get to this page once you're in the lead gen section scroll down and you're looking for six-week slash 42-day challenge ad examples or 42-day detox. Pick whichever one you're going to start with first. The process is going to be the same for both so you can just use the same video applying to whichever funnel you end up choosing to do. I'm going to use the 42-day detox. I'm going to go and click the button for it right there the cold traffic funnel button. It's going to take me to this importing screen so you see the green progress bars loading up. Once it's done it'll show complete view funnel and that means that we have a copy on our account. Now if you aren't signed in it's going to take you to the sign in page first. You'll have to member login. Login with your email address and password and then it's going to take you to this shared funnels page. Once you're here and it has it completed you can click on the view funnel button right there. So the first thing we're going to do is change this part at the end. This is what ClickFunnels calls the path. So it's going to have one for both the landing page and the thank you page. I'll walk you through this process. So on the landing page we want to click this gear icon right here. It says funnel step settings when you move your mouse over it. We want to go and give this a better name for the path. I'm going to call this 42-day detox. Facebook. 2019. Now this is going to be visible to anybody who goes to this page. They're able to see it in the address bar at the top. So make sure you put something that you're okay with somebody else seeing. You do want to try to find something unique and something that tells you what it is. That way in case you just look at the link in the list you can make a pretty good guess of what this link is for. And you also want to use only letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens. So you can do numbers like 42 letters, upper or lowercase. You can do the underscore like you see there or you can use a hyphen. You don't want to use any special symbols or spaces. Stick to that. Once that looks good I'm going to click in here and I'm going to select everything that's in that path box. I'm going to copy it to my clipboard. So it's control C on a Windows computer or command C on a Mac. Once that looks good go and hit the green update funnel step button. I'm going to hit this edit split page test settings. It's going to load up our preview here eventually. It can take some time. Don't worry if it doesn't load up right away. But right here it says edit page split test settings when you move your mouse over that gear icon. When you click on that it will open this page and we want to change the path here as well. So I'm going to click in here where it says path. I'm going to select it all and paste. That should match exactly what we did before. There should be no spaces before or after it. That looks good. I'm going to hit the green update page button. Now I'm going to go to settings and we're going to change a couple things in settings. The first thing I want to do is this path right here. So I'm going to select everything that's in there. I'm going to paste. That's going to put the exact item that we were copying and pasting before for the first page. That way it knows where to start. I also suggest giving it a good name. Something that you'll know what it's for. So if it's for Facebook ads and you did it in March 2019. Whatever you want. This is going to be what it shows up as in your list of all your funnels. It'll make it a lot easier to find it if you need to make changes later. If you give it a good name like that. Next we're going to change the domain. Now we have a bunch of domains here. You probably won't have this many. This gemlegacyteam.com is one that we purchased from ClickFunnels and it's already connected and secure so we know it's good to go. If you haven't already connected your domain with ClickFunnels you do need to go ahead and get that connected. Either buy a new one from them directly which is probably easier or connect your existing one and we have a video that gives you some information on domains up above. I also like to change a group tag. You don't have to change a group tag if you don't want to but by default every single funnel that you import that you get from a shared link it's going to put into this shared funnels group tag and that can mean you'll have a bunch of funnels under the same tag and it's not very useful. So I'm going to go and hit the X next to shared funnels. I'm going to go and type in a new group name. Once I have the name I want typed in there you need to click on it right below to confirm it. You see now it's in that little box that means we're good to go. So we changed the name of the funnel. We set our domain. We pasted the path that we had in our clipboard still from the first step and then we also changed the group. Once everything looks good here I'm going to scroll down to the very bottom and hit save and update settings right there that green button. It's going to take us back to the first landing page and you see now it shows our domain right there. We're actually able to test that so I'm going to hit this little button right here on the right to open it up and test it. There we go. It took us right to that page. Now don't worry about the content here that's something you're going to customize later on we're just worried about the initial setup. So I'm going to go ahead and close this. It's going to take us back to our funnel page. Now I want to go to our thank you page. And now it's already updated the domain right there. You can update the path here as well so you'll hit that gear icon next to a little step settings. I'm going to give it thank you. 42-day detox. And again this is something that can be whatever you want it just has to be letters, numbers, no spaces. You can use underscores, you can use the dashes and this is visible so you want to make sure that you aren't using anything that you don't want anybody to be able to see. I'm going to go and select it all and copy it to my clipboard with that command-seer control seat and then I'm going to hit the green update funnel step button. Right here underneath the page preview I'm going to click on this gear icon that says edit page split test settings when you move your mouse over it. And then inside of path I'm going to select everything that's in there. I'm going to paste, hit update page. Now when I click on that test icon right there I'll take us right to our test page so that looks perfect. We're good to go. One thing I do recommend is if you're using that tracking dock that we got before go ahead and copy your thank you page and paste it onto that tracking dock and the same for your landing page. That way you have one central place where this information is stored and if you need to find it when you're making your Facebook ads or coming back later on and making changes you have this put on your tracking dock. Well that's a basic setup for importing a funnel and getting it ready to customize. I hope you enjoyed this video and hope you have a great rest of your day.
